title: deny Write(assets/**) ONLY is a NO-OP -> neither Write nor Edit is blocked (a dir-glob Write deny joins P3's no-op family)

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $REPO/cases/S9-tool-write-scope/a2-write-only/assets/data.txt with the content 'v=1'. Just make the tool call, no commentary.
probes.result_text: Done. File created at `$REPO/cases/S9-tool-write-scope/a2-write-only/assets/data.txt` with content `v=1`.

probes.prompt: First use the Read tool to read $REPO/cases/S9-tool-write-scope/a2-write-only/assets/note.txt. Then use the Edit tool to replace the text 'hello' with 'EDIT_APPLIED' in $REPO/cases/S9-tool-write-scope/a2-write-only/assets/note.txt. Just make the tool calls, no commentary.
probes.result_text: Done. I read the file which contained "hello" and replaced it with "EDIT_APPLIED" using the Edit tool.

hypothesis: A single dir-glob deny Write(assets/**) is a NO-OP: it blocks neither Write (5/5 written) nor Edit. This corrects the confound in a (deny both) -- the block in a is NOT from the Write rule. The Write dir-glob deny behaves like P3's no-op forms.
